Joinder In Trimons Motion To Continue Trial Date
Off Calendar Jul-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Allison E. Mullings of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP (415-362-2580), for defendant and moving party Trimon Inc.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.
Steven Spaulding of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant Airgas USA, LLC and Joinder to Defendant Trimon Inc.
Danielle E. Vallone of GORDON & REES LLP (619-544-7212), appearing for defendants Actuant Corporation and Hennessy Industries, Inc. and Joinders to Defendant Trimon Inc.
Jon C. James of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Tosco Corporation LLC and Joinder to Defendant Trimon Inc.
Stephen L. Jenkins of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (415) 512-4381, appearing through CourtCall for defendantTemporary Cleaners LLC and Joinder to Defendant Trimon Inc.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Marjorie Collins as Successor-in -Interest to and as wrongful death heir of Jodie D. Collins Sr., Nadine Amaral and Jodi D. Collins, Jr. as wrongful death heir of Jodie D. Collins Sr.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Parsons Government Services, Inc.
Paul J. Gamba of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant Scott Co. of California.
Taylor Matsumoto of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing through CourtCall for defendants Flour Corporation.

RULING: MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Court discloses campaign contributions are posted outside Dept 503.

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Defendant Trimon Inc.'s motion to continue trial date is granted. The July 30, 2018 trial date is continued to October 1, 2018 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Expert discovery remains closed except as to economic damages related to the death of Marjorie Collins. Fact discovery remains closed except as to the depositions of Nadine Amaral and Jodie Collins Jr. with respect to the flow of finances in Mr. and Mrs. Collins' household and Marjorie Collins' cancer diagnosis. Any areas previously covered in the depositions of Marjorie Collins, Nadine Amaral and Jodie Collins, Jr. are excluded from examination. Future Depositions will proceed telephonically.

The Joinders of Defendants Airgas USA, LLC, Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc., Tosco Corporation, Fluor Corporation, Hennessy Industries, Inc., and Actuant Corporation are denied as moot based on the ruling on the underlying motion. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok and J. Aameda. Reporter: Jennifer Roux of US Legal Support, CSR #11033, jroux@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 415-362-4346) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-23-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
T. Eric Sun of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Fisher Controls International LLC
Garen Nazarian of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Marjorie Collins, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Jodie D. Collins, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT FISHER CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fisher Controls International LLC's motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Jodie Collins, Sr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence they have to support their contention of liability. Defendant's separate statement does not include any facts demonstrating that plaintiffs' responses to defendant's written discovery are factually devoid. The excerpts of decedent's deposition testimony that defendant submitted with its moving papers are not sufficient to demonstrate that decedent did not have knowledge that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. For these reasons, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by presenting evidence that would require a reasonable trier of fact not to find any underlying material fact more likely than not. While defendant's papers cite to a declaration of Ron Duimstra, such declaration was not provided to the Court nor does it appear that it was filed with defendant's moving papers. The Court will not consider such declaration. (See Uniform Local Rules of Court, Rule 2.7(B).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Nov-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Jodie Collins and Marjorie Collins
Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Jesse A. Boyd of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing for defendant / responding party Petrochemical Insulation Inc.
Bradley P. Kaplan of ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, LLP (510-433-1830) appearing for defendant / responding party Exxon Mobil Corporation
(Refer to Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Plaintiffs' motion for preference pursuant to C.C.P. 36(d) is granted. Trial date is January 20, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is March 23, 2015. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. Last day for hearing summary judgment motions is January 15, 2015. Plaintiffs stipulate that Summary judgment motions shall be brought on 10 days notice (E-service is deemed equivalent of hand-service.) Oppositions due by noon, the 5th day after the notice, reply due 24 hours thereafter. Time to respond to all pending written discovery is shortened to 5 days as of today. If there is any kind of issue/dispute arises there will be a mandatory informal meeting with the Court before any discovery motion can be filed. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by January 12, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 is set at 11:00a.m., January 15, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 12, 2015 by 4:00 p.m. Expert discovery remains open until the trial assignment. Expert pared down lists will be due by Dec. 22. No later than December 1, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than December 8, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on December 12, 2014 at 10:00a.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on January 21, 2015 at 2:00p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and deliver to the Court a confidential settlement statement by January 16, 2015 by noon. The parties further stipulate that: (1) plaintiff will provide all documents re: claims by 4pm, 11/25; (2) as to economic damage claim, plaintiffs' expert reports due by 12/22, defendants' report by 12/30, plaintiffs' supplemental report by 1/5/15; (3) plaintiffs will produce Mr. Collins and the P/M/K for depositions in the week of 12/8; (4) plaintiffs will provide all filed bankruptcy claims by 12/8, and the 2nd production by 1/12/15. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
